segments.text: Open thy doors, O Lebanon, that the fire may devour thy cedars.
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: Howl, fir tree; for the cedar is fallen; because the mighty are spoiled: howl, O ye oaks of Bashan; for the forest of the vintage is come down.
segments.speaker: Prophet

footnotes: the forest of the vintage is come down: or, the defenced forest

segments.text: There is a voice of the howling of the shepherds; for their glory is spoiled: a voice of the roaring of young lions; for the pride of Jordan is spoiled.
Feed the flock of the slaughter;
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Whose possessors slay them, and hold themselves not guilty: and they that sell them say,
Blessed be the LORD; for I am rich:
segments.speaker: Possessors

segments.text: and their own shepherds pity them not.

I will no more pity the inhabitants of the land, but, lo, I will deliver the men every one into his neighbour's hand, and into the hand of his king: and they shall smite the land, and out of their hand I will not deliver them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

footnotes: deliver: Heb. make to be found

segments.text: And I will feed the flock of slaughter, even you, O poor of the flock. And I took unto me two staves; the one I called Beauty, and the other I called Bands; and I fed the flock.

footnotes: even you, O poor of the flock: or, verily the poor

segments.text: Three shepherds also I cut off in one month; and my soul lothed them, and their soul also abhorred me.

footnotes: lothed: Heb. was straightened for them

segments.text: I will not feed you: that that dieth, let it die; and that that is to be cut off, let it be cut off; and let the rest eat every one the flesh of another.
segments.speaker: Prophet

footnotes: another: Heb. his fellow, or, neighbour

segments.text: And I took my staff, even Beauty, and cut it asunder, that I might break my covenant which I had made with all the people.
And it was broken in that day: and so the poor of the flock that waited upon me knew that it was the word of the LORD.

footnotes: so the poor of the flock, etc. certainly knew

segments.text: If ye think good, give me my price; and if not, forbear.
segments.speaker: Prophet

segments.text: So they weighed for my price thirty pieces of silver.

footnotes: If ye think good: Heb. If it be good in your eyes

segments.text: Cast it unto the potter: a goodly price that I was prised at of them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, and cast them to the potter in the house of the LORD.
Then I cut asunder mine other staff, even Bands, that I might break the brotherhood between Judah and Israel.
Take unto thee yet the instruments of a foolish shepherd.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will raise up a shepherd in the land, which shall not visit those that be cut off, neither shall seek the young one, nor heal that that is broken, nor feed that that standeth still: but he shall eat the flesh of the fat, and tear their claws in pieces.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Woe to the idol shepherd that leaveth the flock! the sword shall be upon his arm, and upon his right eye: his arm shall be clean dried up, and his right eye shall be utterly darkened.
segments.speaker: Prophet
